data.title: Web Framework
data.description: Adobe.com is built using a combination of modern web technologies.
data.technical_spec: Uses a combination of HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript frameworks like React or Angular.

data.title: Backend Infrastructure
data.description: Utilizes a robust backend structure to handle vast amounts of data and user requests.
data.technical_spec: Primarily employs cloud services, possibly leveraging Adobe Experience Manager (AEM).

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Implements CDN to ensure swift content delivery across global regions.
data.technical_spec: Employs CDNs such as Akamai or AWS CloudFront.

data.title: Database Management
data.description: Supports complex querying and data storage through advanced database systems.
data.technical_spec: Likely utilizes SQL and NoSQL databases for flexibility and scalability.

data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Facilitates change and scaling through microservices.
data.technical_spec: Implemented using Docker and Kubernetes for containerization.
